name: Interleukin 33
summary: IL33 encodes interleukin-33, a dual-function cytokine and nuclear factor belonging to the IL-1 family. IL-33 functions as an epithelial alarmin: it is constitutively expressed in barrier tissues (lung, skin, gut) and released upon cell damage or stress, acting as a danger signal to activate innate and adaptive immune cells. Full-length IL-33 binds the ST2 (IL1RL1) receptor on group 2 innate lymphoid cells (ILC2s), mast cells, Th2 lymphocytes, and eosinophils, driving type 2 immunity. Proteolytic cleavage by allergens or inflammatory proteases generates mature forms with enhanced activity. Asthma-associated GWAS variants regulate IL33 expression through an enhancer-blocking element ~2.3 kb upstream, with differential OCT-1 (POU2F1) binding. IL-33 is upregulated by PM2.5, allergens, and viral infections, positioning it at the interface of environmental exposure and genetic susceptibility in asthma.
regulatory_notes: Asthma-associated variants (e.g., rs1888909) lie in a regulatory region 2.3 kb upstream; chromatin conformation capture shows looping to IL33 promoter. Strong eQTL in lung and bronchial epithelium (GTEx v8). Promoter contains NF-κB and AP-1 binding sites; IL33 expression induced by NF-κB activation.

expression_context.notes: Constitutive in stromal and epithelial cells
Upregulated by PM2.5, allergens, viral infection

mechanistic_hypotheses: PM2.5-induced oxidative stress and NF-κB activation drive IL33 transcription and release from bronchial epithelium; genetic variants increasing basal IL33 expression amplify this response, explaining G×E interaction.
IL-33 released from damaged epithelial cells activates ILC2s and mast cells via ST2; sustained release under chronic PM2.5 exposure promotes eosinophilic inflammation and airway hyperresponsiveness.

Asthma-risk alleles at the IL33 enhancer increase IL-33 protein in plasma and airway; this genetically determined baseline modulates susceptibility to environmental triggers including allergens and pollution.

references.title: Asthma-associated genetic variants induce IL33 differential expression through an enhancer-blocking regulatory region

IL-33 and the intestine: the role in intestinal inflammation and gut immunity
